description_long: This object is a tall coconut palm tree with a textured, segmented brown trunk that tapers slightly as it rises. The trunk is covered in ring-like scars, typical of palm trees, and supports a dense crown of long, arching fronds. The fronds are composed of numerous narrow, pointed leaflets that radiate outward, creating a lush, umbrella-like canopy. The leaves are primarily green with some subtle variations in shade, and the overall structure is robust and lifelike. No visible coconuts or fruit clusters are present among the fronds, and the base of the trunk is not rooted in any visible ground or soil.
description: A tall coconut palm tree with a segmented brown trunk and a dense crown of long, green fronds. The leaves radiate outward, forming a lush, umbrella-like canopy.
description_view_0: The palm tree is seen at an angle, highlighting the curvature of the trunk and the spread of the fronds to the right.
description_view_1: A direct front view shows the symmetrical arrangement of the fronds and the straight alignment of the trunk.
description_view_2: The tree is viewed from a slightly different angle, emphasizing the leftward lean of the trunk and the layered fronds.
description_view_3: A straight-on view of the trunk with the fronds radiating evenly, giving a clear look at the segmented trunk pattern.
attribute_husk_pattern: The trunk displays a segmented, ringed texture with horizontal banding and rough, fibrous markings. The fronds have a dense, linear arrangement of narrow leaflets with visible vein patterns.
attribute_proximity_to_palm_trees: The object itself is a palm tree; no other palm trees are visible nearby.
